messages.text: Heads up team — we're seeing a spike in UPI payment failures since 9:30 AM. Error rate jumped from 0.8% to 4.2%. Investigating now.
I see it too. Most failures are on the HDFC Bank side — getting `BANK_SERVER_ERROR` responses. Should we route traffic to the backup PSP?
Not yet. Let me check if NPCI has posted anything on their status page. Last time this happened, it was a scheduled maintenance window we missed.

Just checked — NPCI status page shows all green. But I found this in the Razorpay dashboard: they're throttling us because we exceeded the rate limit on the /payments/validate endpoint. We're sending 2x the normal volume because of the sale.
That explains it. @sneha.patel can you implement the exponential backoff on the retry logic? We discussed this in ADR-007 but never shipped it.
On it. I'll use a base delay of 500ms with max 3 retries and a jitter factor. Should have a PR up by 2 PM.
Update: Sneha's PR is merged and deployed to staging. Error rate in staging dropped back to 0.6%. Deploying to production at 3 PM after the deployment window opens.
Good catch team. Let's add a Grafana alert for rate limit responses so we catch this earlier next time. @deepak.gupta can you add that to the SRE backlog?
Already created the Jira ticket: SRE-342. Will set up the alert this week.
Team, I've been evaluating vector databases for the VaultIQ project. Narrowed it down to Qdrant vs pgvector vs Weaviate. Here's my analysis:

Qdrant: Best hybrid search (dense + sparse native), easy to self-host, great filtering. No SQL though.
pgvector: Lives inside Postgres, familiar, but no native hybrid search and slower at scale.
Weaviate: Full-featured but heavy, needs more infra. Overkill for our use case.
I'd vote Qdrant. We already run it in the Marketplace team for product embeddings and it's been rock solid. 2M vectors, 99.9% uptime, P99 latency under 50ms.
One concern with Qdrant — if we need joins with structured metadata (like org charts, employee tables), we'd need a separate SQL store anyway. pgvector avoids that extra hop.

Good point @fatima. But for VaultIQ, the metadata filtering in Qdrant (payload filters) should handle most of our ACL and source-type filtering. We don't need SQL joins on the vector store — that logic lives in the application layer.
From an MLOps perspective, Qdrant also has better snapshot and backup support. We can version our vector collections alongside model versions. My vote is Qdrant.
Decision: going with Qdrant. I'll write up ADR-001 for this. Thanks everyone for the input.

Reminder: we're upgrading Python from 3.11 to 3.12 across all services next week. Key changes:
1. Better error messages
2. `per-interpreter GIL` (experimental)
3. Some deprecated modules removed (distutils, etc.)

Please test your service locally with 3.12 by Thursday.
Payment service tested — all 342 tests pass on 3.12. One deprecation warning in the `xml.etree` usage but it's non-critical.
Mobile BFF has an issue — the `aiohttp` version we use (3.8.x) doesn't support 3.12 cleanly. Need to upgrade to 3.9+. I'll handle the PR.
Thanks @priyanka. Please update the requirements.txt AND the Dockerfile base image. Our base image is `python:3.11-slim` — I've already pushed `python:3.12-slim` to our internal registry.
Infra note: our CI runners already have 3.12 installed. No changes needed on the pipeline side. I've updated the base Docker image in the shared Helm chart.

Weekly hiring update:

**Staff ML Platform Engineer:** 3 candidates in pipeline. 1 cleared the system design round — Manisha Verma from Flipkart. Final round with Sanjay scheduled for Tuesday.

**Senior Payments Engineer (2 positions):** Posted on LinkedIn + Instahyre. 47 applications received, 12 shortlisted for phone screen.

**GenAI Engineer:** This is hard. Very few candidates with production RAG experience. Only 5 applications so far. Considering increasing the salary band from 18-25 LPA to 22-30 LPA.
For the GenAI role, I'd support the band increase. Good GenAI engineers are being offered 35+ at Series B startups. We need to be competitive. @sunita.reddy can you approve the revised band?
Approved. Update the JD with the new band. Also, let's add "experience with RAG, vector databases, and LLM evaluation" as preferred qualifications. The market has matured — we can be specific now.
Done. Updated JD posted on all channels. I'll also reach out to our referral network — offering ₹50K referral bonus for GenAI hires.
🔴 ALERT: P0 — Database connection pool exhausted on payments-db-primary. Active connections: 200/200. Queued: 47. Alert source: Grafana.
IC here. Investigating. @amit.joshi are you available?
Yes, just woke up. Checking the connection pool metrics. We had a similar issue 3 months ago — it was a connection leak in the settlement batch job.

Found it. The nightly reconciliation job that runs at 2 AM opened 80 connections and never released them. It's the new code from PR #1247 — the connection cleanup in the `finally` block was accidentally removed during refactoring.

Immediate action: I'm killing the stuck connections manually.
```sql
SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = 'idle in transaction' AND query_start < now() - interval '30 minutes';
```
This freed 73 connections. Pool is recovering.
I've pushed a hotfix to restore the `finally` block. PR #1289 — needs urgent review. Also adding a connection timeout of 30 seconds to prevent this class of bug.
PR reviewed and approved. Deploying hotfix now. Connection pool stable at 45/200. Marking incident as MONITORING.

All clear. Connection pool healthy for 45 minutes. Marking incident as RESOLVED. PIR scheduled for Thursday 3 PM.

**Impact:** ~15 minutes of degraded payment processing. 23 transactions failed and auto-retried successfully.
**Root cause:** Connection leak in nightly reconciliation job (PR #1247).
**Fix:** Restored connection cleanup + added timeout (PR #1289).

Q3 planning starts next week. I need input from all team leads on:
1. What carried over from Q2 that we didn't finish?
2. What new features are customers asking for most?
3. Any tech debt that's slowing us down?

Please reply in this thread by Wednesday EOD.

Marketplace carryover:
- Dynamic pricing engine (was P2, bumping to P1 — competitor launched theirs)
- Multi-language listings (pushed to Q3)

Customer requests: Better search filters, saved searches, price drop alerts

Tech debt: Our Elasticsearch cluster is on 7.x — need to migrate to 8.x. Also, the recommendation model retraining pipeline is manual and flaky.

Payments:
- Juspay migration at 50% — need to complete the remaining 50% in Q3
- International payments (Stripe) pushed from Q2

Customer asks: EMI options on UPI, auto-debit for subscriptions

Tech debt: Payment retry logic is scattered across 3 services — needs consolidation into a central retry service. Also the settlement reconciliation is still a cron job — should be event-driven.

GenAI:
- VaultIQ (internal RAG) is on track for July 31 launch
- Customer support copilot planned for Q3

Biggest concern: LLM costs. We're spending ₹8L/month on API calls during development. Need to implement caching and model cascading before we go to production. Budget approved for ₹15L/month in production but I think we can get it under ₹10L with proper optimization.

Security audit findings from the Q2 external audit are in. Summary:

✅ 0 Critical findings
⚠️ 2 High findings
📝 5 Medium findings

High findings:
1. API keys for 3 internal services found in Git history (rotated already, but we need to implement pre-commit hooks)
2. The staging environment has no WAF — it's publicly accessible and has real-ish test data
For #1 — I'll set up `gitleaks` as a pre-commit hook this week. Will also add it to CI as a blocking check. No PR should merge if secrets are detected.
For #2 — staging WAF is a quick win. I'll configure AWS WAF on the staging ALB by Friday. Also, let's replace all real-ish test data with synthetic data. We have Faker scripts for this.
Thanks team. Remediation deadline is July 15. I need confirmation that both high findings are resolved by then. Please update the audit tracker in Confluence.

🎉 Happy to announce BigCorp's Annual Hackathon 2026! Theme: "AI for Internal Productivity"

Dates: July 18-19 (Friday-Saturday)
Team size: 2-4 people
Prizes: ₹2L for 1st place, ₹1L for 2nd, ₹50K for 3rd

Past winners built amazing things — last year's winning project (auto-ticket-triage) is now in production!

Sign up in #hackathon-2026 by July 10.
Count me in! Anyone from DevEx want to team up? I'm thinking we could build an AI code reviewer that integrates with our PR workflow.
I'm in! Was thinking the same thing. Let's also add auto-generated test suggestions. @karthik.venkat want to join? You've got the LLM chops.
Absolutely. I'll bring the RAG and prompt engineering. Team name suggestion: "The Linters" 😄
